title: MORALE Cross-Species Transcription-Factor Binding Benchmark

data_urls.access_notes: Archive for the MORALE cross-species TF binding study.
Use to recover preprocessing, species/TF lists, and benchmark splits.

access.authentication: none_expected_for_code_archive; upstream ENCODE/ChIP resources may vary
instructions: Use the repository/archive to identify species, transcription factors, cell types, coordinates, labels, and original source resources.
Start with one transcription factor and one source-target species pair before expanding to the full benchmark.
Record genome build, coordinate system, sequence window size, species, TF, cell type/tissue, binding label, and split.

scale.download_size: variable multi-species regulatory genomics resource; start with a selected TF/species pair

recommended_axes.name: species_similarity
recommended_axes.definition: Evolutionary or phylogenetic distance between source and target species.

recommended_axes.name: motif_and_tf_family_support
recommended_axes.definition: Support in motif syntax, TF family, motif occurrence, and motif grammar relative to training sequences.

recommended_axes.name: sequence_composition_and_repeat_support
recommended_axes.definition: k-mer, GC, CpG, low-complexity, and repeat-family support across species.

recommended_axes.name: orthologous_locus_support
recommended_axes.definition: Whether target loci align to or are orthologous with source-species regulatory loci.

useful_for: Testing whether regulatory sequence models learn species-invariant TF binding rules or species-specific sequence artifacts.
Providing a DART-like external biology axis for DNA foundation-model generalization audits.
Contrasting sequence-similarity, motif-syntax, and species-distance explanations.
leakage_risks: Orthologous or lifted-over loci can leak equivalent regulatory examples across species.
Repeat-family and GC composition can masquerade as regulatory mechanism.
TF identity and motif family can dominate if target TFs overlap too strongly with training TFs.

paperclip_provenance.notes: Paperclip surfaced cross-species TF binding as a recurring regulatory-genomics generalization setting; official repository and Zenodo archive provide a portable entry point.
